_note: Rolling record of each PC's experience in cleared rooms. Orchestrator writes one entry per PC after each room clear. Keep the 5 most recent entries per PC. Fiction language only — no mechanical terms.

maren.entry: Two skeletons met us at the threshold — old things, hollow of everything but reflex. My faith drove one apart before it could close the distance; Sable's blade finished the other. The entrance holds, but whatever disturbed this place came through here first, and it knew what it was looking for.

The burial corridor had ghouls — three of them, pressing out from the wall niches, with the hunger of things that have been waiting. I laid a wasting curse across their kind before the first blow fell. Sable's divine fire sent two ghouls and the skeleton fleeing south in terror. The one that held its ground I destroyed with a word of power, though the effort cost me. The corridor holds.

The offering vault held something worse — a specter, incorporeal and cold, that drifted straight toward me and reached for me twice. Both times its touch found nothing. I drove it back with sacred fire and a conjured weapon that hung in the air like a second will until the spirit recognised it was losing and fled through the south wall. The shambling dead it left behind, Sable and I unmade. Whatever that thing is, it is ahead of us now, and it is not finished.

sable.entry: Two skeletons at the threshold. Maren's channelling drove one to pieces before I reached it; I put down the second. Fast work, nothing that should worry us — but something opened this tomb before we did, and it came through the same door we did, and it knew what it wanted.

The corridor was tight and there were three ghouls and a skeleton in it, all hungry. I called on my oath and pushed two of the ghouls and the skeleton back with holy fire — they fled south, ahead of us. Maren finished the last one with a single word. My divine reserves are empty now. What comes next, I meet with steel and whatever courage is left.

The vault had a specter — something without a body, made of cold light and reach — and it wanted Maren. She held it off twice and drove it back with fire and a conjured blade until it ran through solid stone ahead of us, wounded but not finished. I cleared the shambling dead while she worked. That spirit is in the next room. It has had time to collect itself.
